title: helianthemum vulgare
intro: Helianthemum vulgare, also known as Rockrose, is a low-growing, flowering plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of colds and bronchitis. Helianthemum vulgare is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, where it can be combined with other herbs to enhance its effects and provide relief from various health issues.
